topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara scrolled through her feed, mindlessly flicking through updates from friends and distant acquaintances. Then, a notification popped up. It was a post from Mr. Harrison, her notoriously strict AP History teacher. The photo was a slightly blurry snapshot taken outside the school library, and the caption was a cryptic comment about a historical event they had just discussed in class. Elara paused, her thumb hovering over the 'like' button. She reread the post. It seemed innocuous enough, perhaps a casual observation. But the familiarity of the setting, the subtle nod to their shared academic space, sent a strange ripple through her chest. She minimized the app, feeling a sudden, tight knot forming beneath her ribs. Why was this person, someone who kept a professional distance, sharing moments that felt… too intimate? She stared at her phone screen, the light reflecting in her wide eyes, unable to quite place the feeling bubbling up inside her.

[story 2]

Liam was organizing his digital files when he stumbled across it. A profile picture of Ms. Evans, his English literature instructor, was overlaid on a picture of a dimly lit coffee shop. The caption mentioned a book they had both recommended last week. Liam leaned back in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight. He squinted at the image, his brow furrowing deeply. He clicked on the profile, navigating through her posts—mostly innocuous pictures of books and garden scenes. But then he saw the timestamp: it was exactly the time he had left the campus café after their final session. A slow, unsteady breath escaped him. He tapped the screen repeatedly, his fingers tracing the edge of the glass. A distinct sense of disorientation washed over him; the structure of things felt suddenly askew. He couldn't shake the sensation that he was observing something entirely unexpected, something that didn't fit the established boundaries of their relationship.

[story 3]

Maya was waiting for the bus, fiddling with the strap of her backpack. Her phone buzzed incessantly. It was a direct message from Professor Davies, her philosophy professor. The message contained a link to an obscure online forum, and attached was a photograph of a university campus landmark, taken from a vantage point only accessible from the faculty parking lot. Maya blinked, rereading the message three times. Her jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. She looked around the bus stop, suddenly hyper-aware of every passing student, every hurried commuter. A strange pressure built behind her eyes, making the world seem slightly too sharp. She felt a distinct need to look away, to disengage, yet her gaze kept drifting back to the screen, analyzing the context of the shared image, searching for a logical explanation that simply wasn't there.
